oldMatchDict.docstring:
Compute average clustering coefficient.

A clustering coefficient for the whole graph is the average, 

.. math::

   C = \frac{1}{n}\sum_{v \in G} c_v,
   
where `n` is the number of nodes in `G`.

Parameters
----------
G : graph
   A networkx graph
weighted : bool, optional
   If True use weights on edges in computing clustering coefficients.

Returns
-------
out : float
   Average clustering

Examples
--------
>>> G=nx.complete_graph(5)
>>> print(nx.average_clustering(G))
1.0

Notes
-----
This is a space saving routine; it might be faster
to use clustering to get a list and then take the average.

Self loops are ignored.

References
----------
.. [1] Generalizations of the clustering coefficient to weighted 
   complex networks by J. Saramäki, M. Kivelä, J.-P. Onnela, 
   K. Kaski, and J. Kertész, Physical Review E, 75 027105 (2007).  
   http://jponnela.com/web_documents/a9.pdf
oldMatchDict.sourceCode:
def average_clustering(G,weighted=False):
    r"""Compute average clustering coefficient.

    A clustering coefficient for the whole graph is the average, 

    .. math::

       C = \frac{1}{n}\sum_{v \in G} c_v,
       
    where `n` is the number of nodes in `G`.

    Parameters
    ----------
    G : graph
       A networkx graph
    weighted : bool, optional
       If True use weights on edges in computing clustering coefficients.

    Returns
    -------
    out : float
       Average clustering
    
    Examples
    --------
    >>> G=nx.complete_graph(5)
    >>> print(nx.average_clustering(G))
    1.0

    Notes
    -----
    This is a space saving routine; it might be faster
    to use clustering to get a list and then take the average.

    Self loops are ignored.

    References
    ----------
    .. [1] Generalizations of the clustering coefficient to weighted 
       complex networks by J. Saramäki, M. Kivelä, J.-P. Onnela, 
       K. Kaski, and J. Kertész, Physical Review E, 75 027105 (2007).  
       http://jponnela.com/web_documents/a9.pdf
    """
    order=G.order()
    s=sum(clustering(G,weighted=weighted).values())
    return s/float(order)

newMatchDict.docstring:
Compute the average clustering coefficient for the graph G.

The clustering coefficient for the graph is the average, 

.. math::

   C = \frac{1}{n}\sum_{v \in G} c_v,
   
where `n` is the number of nodes in `G`.

Parameters
----------
G : graph

nodes : container of nodes, optional (default=all nodes in G)
   Compute average clustering for nodes in this container. 

weight : string or None, optional (default=None)
   The edge attribute that holds the numerical value used as a weight.
   If None, then each edge has weight 1.

count_zeros : bool (default=False)       
   If False include only the nodes with nonzero clustering in the average.

Returns
-------
avg : float
   Average clustering

Examples
--------
>>> G=nx.complete_graph(5)
>>> print(nx.average_clustering(G))
1.0

Notes
-----
This is a space saving routine; it might be faster
to use the clustering function to get a list and then take the average.

Self loops are ignored.

References
----------
.. [1] Generalizations of the clustering coefficient to weighted 
   complex networks by J. Saramäki, M. Kivelä, J.-P. Onnela, 
   K. Kaski, and J. Kertész, Physical Review E, 75 027105 (2007).  
   http://jponnela.com/web_documents/a9.pdf
.. [2] Marcus Kaiser,  Mean clustering coefficients: the role of isolated 
   nodes and leafs on clustering measures for small-world networks.
   http://arxiv.org/abs/0802.2512
newMatchDict.sourceCode:
def average_clustering(G, nodes=None, weight=None, count_zeros=True):
    r"""Compute the average clustering coefficient for the graph G.

    The clustering coefficient for the graph is the average, 

    .. math::

       C = \frac{1}{n}\sum_{v \in G} c_v,
       
    where `n` is the number of nodes in `G`.

    Parameters
    ----------
    G : graph

    nodes : container of nodes, optional (default=all nodes in G)
       Compute average clustering for nodes in this container. 

    weight : string or None, optional (default=None)
       The edge attribute that holds the numerical value used as a weight.
       If None, then each edge has weight 1.

    count_zeros : bool (default=False)       
       If False include only the nodes with nonzero clustering in the average.

    Returns
    -------
    avg : float
       Average clustering
    
    Examples
    --------
    >>> G=nx.complete_graph(5)
    >>> print(nx.average_clustering(G))
    1.0

    Notes
    -----
    This is a space saving routine; it might be faster
    to use the clustering function to get a list and then take the average.

    Self loops are ignored.

    References
    ----------
    .. [1] Generalizations of the clustering coefficient to weighted 
       complex networks by J. Saramäki, M. Kivelä, J.-P. Onnela, 
       K. Kaski, and J. Kertész, Physical Review E, 75 027105 (2007).  
       http://jponnela.com/web_documents/a9.pdf
    .. [2] Marcus Kaiser,  Mean clustering coefficients: the role of isolated 
       nodes and leafs on clustering measures for small-world networks.
       http://arxiv.org/abs/0802.2512
    """
    c=clustering(G,nodes,weight=weight).values()
    if not count_zeros:
        c = [v for v in c if v > 0]
    return sum(c)/float(len(c))
